title: Insane Tinfoil Hat Theory Nausicaa and Evangelion
display_title: Insane Tinfoil Hat Theory Nausicaa and Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Insane Tinfoil Hat Theory: Nausicaa and Evangelion...             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Apr 30, 2013 3:46 pm Now, a word of warning: This may be the dumbest theory ever posted on the internet. I thought it up in the shower, so be gentle. Now, this is the theory: Nausicaa of the Valley of the Wind and Neon Genesis Evangelion take place in the same universe. Now the crux of my argument comes down to the God Warriors (which were, incidentally, animated by Hideaki Anno). In many ways, they look very similar to Evangelion units without armour. This is less apparent in the original movie due to the lack of detailed shots and relatively simple animation, but it is much more apparent in the God Warrior from the mini-featurette Anno directed (the one shown in cinemas alongside Evangelion 3.0), which is quite obviously the same creature, sharing the proportions, head design, eyes, teeth, breath weapon, and even the Spears of Light. For reference, here's a picture of a statuette the God Warrior (featurette version): SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Tue Apr 30, 2013 4:47 pm The parallels between Nausicaa and Eva have been drawn many times, and Anno is noted to have taken some inspiration from it. To quote Xard from this post: Xard wrote: Not long ago Anno told Toshio Suzuki that Eva was his attempt at Nausicaa fanfiction since Miyazaki didn't let him direct sequel to original film. Of course given how liberally Anno lifted stuff from the manga that isn't exactly news but still...

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Apr 30, 2013 4:51 pm Paranoid wrote: Of course he could never do anything official with that without having Miyazaki's permission, which I doubt he'd get.

[]:             Quote Post by EVAfacepalm » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:33 pm You know, I sometimes wonder what it would be like if Miyazaki directed an Evangelion movie. Probably a lot more emotional recovery for the characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:22 pm EVAfacepalm wrote: You know, I sometimes wonder what it would be like if Miyazaki directed an Evangelion movie.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:44 pm EVAfacepalm wrote: You know, I sometimes wonder what it would be like if Miyazaki directed an Evangelion movie.

[]:             Quote Post by VirtualBathsalt » Tue Apr 30, 2013 7:42 pm EVAfacepalm wrote: You know, I sometimes wonder what it would be like if Miyazaki directed an Evangelion movie

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Wed May 01, 2013 1:07 am I kinda like this crackpot theory, actually, except the "Shinji is Naushika" part, that's going a bit too far for continued disbelief-suspension.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 01, 2013 1:11 am LeoXiao wrote: I kinda like this crackpot theory, actually, except the "Shinji is Naushika" part, that's going a bit too far for continued disbelief-suspension.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Wed May 01, 2013 2:37 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: All of the Evangelion would have wings and be able to fly, because Miyazaki isn't interested in things that aren't airborne. Rei wouldn't be anywhere in the series as of yet, Gendo would have been dead prior to the story's beginning, and Shinji would be an orphan looking for adventure. Asuka would basically be the perfect house-wife once she matures into a woman, and she would be living with her mother Yui who is pregnant with a little girl. (I wonder who could that be???) Kaji would be Asuka's dad, and Misato would be the cool aunt, sister of Yui, and take Asuka and Shinji on fun little shopping trips or something. Fuyutsuki would have a big bushy mustache and live next door to Asuka. Then one day the Angels came, seeking to end the world by reconnecting with Asuka, who is a descendant of a royal bloodline from outer-space. The Angels would seek to make Asuka their new queen over the world they're about to conquer, because the Angels were the soldiers that defended the ancient galactic kingdom Asuka's family tree once ruled over. This would make Asuka a long-lost princess, and we know how much Miyazaki loves those!

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed May 01, 2013 5:52 am LeoXiao wrote: I kinda like this crackpot theory, actually, except the "Shinji is Naushika" part, that's going a bit too far for continued disbelief-suspension.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed May 01, 2013 5:55 am Note to self - must watch Nausicaa . Oh, look; I already have a copy waiting.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed May 01, 2013 6:38 am Nausicaä was one of Anno's biggest influence if not arguably the biggest. The similarities are apparent and since he was denied by Miyazaki to play "war games" with Kushana (if you read the manga, you are going to realise where Asuka comes from, why the Nerv HQ is a pyramid, entire Nadia aesthetics etc) so he wrote his own fanfic that turned into Evangelion. Though I'd say the chances of them playing in the same universe are as high as Star Wars playing 2 million years after Kurosawa's Hidden Fortress.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed May 01, 2013 10:18 am Fireball wrote: Nausicaä was one of Anno's biggest influence if not arguably the biggest. The similarities are apparent and since he was denied by Miyazaki to play "war games" with Kushana (if you read the manga, you are going to realise where Asuka comes from, why the Nerv HQ is a pyramid, entire Nadia aesthetics etc) so he wrote his own fanfic that turned into Evangelion. Though I'd say the chances of them playing in the same universe are as high as Star Wars playing 2 million years after Kurosawa's Hidden Fortress.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed May 01, 2013 11:15 am They aren't that close thematically (fanfic is just a facetious description). Nausicaä is Miyazaki being Miyazaki trying to teach to live in harmony with nature thrown in some spiritual sci-fi and plane combat in the blender and people trying to revive ancient weapons (well I guess that's close enough) and aesthetically speaking, I would have to dissect manga pages which are copyrighted and which I can't be arsed. If one really wants to grasp NGE's roots one has the read the manga.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed May 01, 2013 1:24 pm VirtualBathsalt wrote: Pen-Pen would get a huge increase in screen time.

[]: Re: Insane Tinfoil Hat Theory: Nausicaa and Evangelion...             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Wed May 01, 2013 1:47 pm Squigsquasher wrote: and that the vast fungus forests are the reconstituted forms of the hundreds of thousands that "died" (a loose term regarding Instrumentality, hence the quotation marks).

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed May 01, 2013 3:59 pm I am partial to the theory that Sandworms are Fruit of Life-bearing beings, making the Spice LCL and that the Bene Gesserit are a revised continuation of Seele after the marked failure of Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Maru » Fri May 03, 2013 1:35 pm I have to agree with Fireball that Asuka is very clearly a Kushana expy; she shares much in common with both the manga and anime iterations of the character. Like Asuka, the manga's Kushana had a mother who went insane trying to save her daughter, and with whom she has a strained relationship. They are both military people (or in Asuka's case, she thinks of herself as military), and they have both been training for war for a long time. They also both have asshole fathers who made their lives miserable. The anime's Kushana was missing an arm from when she was savaged by an insect, and there were probably far worse, less visible scars, considering her remark that "the man who marries me will see far worse than this [referring to her missing arm]." And we all remember too well what happened to Asuka in EoE. Even superficially they look similar; they are both depicted as redheads. I don't think Nausicaa is necessarily a reincarnation of Shinji, although that could sort of make sense; it fits better with the anime's plot, in which the theme of Nausicaa as a messiah figure was given greater focus, with the prophecy of the "blue clad one" (which is curious, since Rei is the character most closely associated with the color blue). If anything though, I would say it is more likely that Shinji's soul, or an impression of it, was left on Unit One, which became the God Warrior in the manga. It would explain the God Warrior's personality, particularly how he fixates on Nausicaa as a mother figure. The only flaw with this theory is that when the engineers of Pejitei found the God Warrior, it was apparently factory new, and had supposedly never been used before. Lavinius wrote: I am partial to the theory that Sandworms are Fruit of Life-bearing beings, making the Spice LCL and that the Bene Gesserit are a revised continuation of Seele after the marked failure of Instrumentality.

Article: Insane Tinfoil Hat Theory Nausicaa and Evangelion
 have to agree with Fireball that Asuka is very clearly a Kushana expy; she shares much in common with both the manga and anime iterations of the character. Like Asuka, the manga's Kushana had a mother who went insane trying to save her daughter, and with whom she has a strained relationship. They are both military people (or in Asuka's case, she thinks of herself as military), and they have both been training for war for a long time. They also both have asshole fathers who made their lives miserable. The anime's Kushana was missing an arm from when she was savaged by an insect, and there were probably far worse, less visible scars, considering her remark that "the man who marries me will see far worse than this [referring to her missing arm]." And we all remember too well what happened to Asuka in EoE. Even superficially they look similar; they are both depicted as redheads. I don't think Nausicaa is necessarily a reincarnation of Shinji, although that could sort of make sense; it fits better with the anime's plot, in which the theme of Nausicaa as a messiah figure was given greater focus, with the prophecy of the "blue clad one" (which is curious, since Rei is the character most closely associated with the color blue). If anything though, I would say it is more likely that Shinji's soul, or an impression of it, was left on Unit One, which became the God Warrior in the manga. It would explain the God Warrior's personality, particularly how he fixates on Nausicaa as a mother figure. The only flaw with this theory is that when the engineers of Pejitei found the God Warrior, it was apparently factory new, and had supposedly never been used before. Lavinius wrote: I am partial to the theory that Sandworms are Fruit of Life-bearing beings, making the Spice LCL and that the Bene Gesserit are a revised continuation of Seele after the marked failure of Instrumentality.
